summary: The doc_build_status_check job is a status gate that checks upstream job outcomes. The conditional script evaluates to false because an upstream job has status "failure" instead of "success" or "skipped", causing the status check to exit with code 1.
root_cause: The doc_build_status_check job detected that an upstream job in the 'Build documentation' workflow has failed (status: "failure"). The job contains a conditional script that requires all upstream jobs to have status "success" or "skipped", and it exits with code 1 when this condition is not met.

evidence.reason: The conditional script shows the status check is comparing literal status strings; 'failure' does not equal 'success' or 'skipped', so the first condition evaluates to false
When the condition fails, the else branch is executed, causing the job to exit with code 1
Confirms the job failed due to the exit 1 command being executed
This job name indicates it is a status check that validates the outcome of upstream documentation build jobs

suggested_fix: Review the logs of the upstream documentation build jobs in the 'Build documentation' workflow to identify why they failed. The doc_build_status_check job is functioning correctly by detecting and propagating the upstream failure.

model_info._backfill_note: Pre-Codex-2026-05-14 F2 fix. The Claude shim now persists _model_info from every API call; this row predates that emit so resolved_model and usage are not recoverable. requested_model is set to the shim-level alias (haiku for v1, sonnet for v2) which is what the post-fix shim emits.
